subject: [PATCH] mm/mempolicy: fix memory leak in weighted_interleave_auto_store()

reviews.author: Joshua Hahn
reviews.summary: Reviewer Joshua Hahn clarified that his previous patch rejection was not intended to imply the original patch was incorrect, but rather he should have been more cautious in choosing the name 'auto' due to its overlap with a C keyword.
reviews.raw_body:
(I just want to clarify that I am not suggesting that 6cce897a37dc was
incorrect. In hindsight, I should have been more wary about using a name
like "auto" when it is already a C keyword)

2026-03-31.patch_summary: This patch fixes a memory leak in the weighted_interleave_auto_store() function by properly releasing resources when an error occurs, preventing memory from being allocated but not freed.

reviews.author: Jackie Liu
reviews.summary: reviewer requested a re-review of their own patch, sent as a follow-up to the original patch
reviews.raw_body:
Thank you. I quickly wrote a patch and sent it. Sashiko looks amazing.

The link is https://lore.kernel.org/all/20260401005702.7096-1-liu.yun@linux.dev/

Please help me review it again.

-- 
Jackie Liu

2026-04-01.patch_summary: This patch fixes a memory leak in the weighted_interleave_auto_store() function by properly releasing resources when an error occurs, preventing memory from being allocated but not freed.
